publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2026-03-23 23:59:06 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Collection documenting the experiences of Kathryn Elizabeth Yohn (née Altemeier), Cornell University Class of 1928, during her time as a student. The collection includes materials on her academic career; social life, including membership in Alpha Omicron Pi sorority; and participation in Cornell University athletics. Also present are materials documenting student life at Cornell, including musical and theatrical performances as well as annual events including Spring Day. Collection contains scrapbook, correspondence, programs, photographs, programs, publications, and ephemera.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Collection is arranged into six thematic series: I. Personal, II. University Events, III. Performances, IV. Athletics, V. Student Life, and VI. Ephemera. Series are arranged by size, with standard-sized items in letter- and legal-sized folders and smaller items in white envelopes. Items are next arranged chronologically. Undated items are arranged alphabetically by title. Scrapbook and oversized items from Series I and V are stored in Box 1.

bioghist.head: Biographical Note
bioghist.p: Kathryn Elizabeth Altemeier was born on February 17, 1906, in Port Jervis, NY to Augustus P. Altemeier and Edna Nicholls Altemeier. She graduated from Cornell University in 1928 with a Bachelor of Arts in Education. While at Cornell, Altemeier was active in athletics and social activities on campus. She was a member of the women's basketball, rowing, and rifle teams. For her skill as a member of the Cornell Women's Rifle Team, she was elected to Dot and Circle, a national honor society for women rifle shooters. She also held leadership positions with the basketball and crew teams and was vice president of the Cornell Women's Athletic Association.

Altemeier was a member of the Alpha Omicron Pi sorority and served as president of the Alpha Omicron Pi house for the academic year 1927-1928. She participated in the Dance Festival and served as a member of the Class Picnic Committee, Hades Committee, and Sophomore Cotillion Committee. Altemeier married Samuel H. Yohn in 1932. She worked as a physical education teacher for the Bound Brook, NJ school district until her retirement. Kathryn Yohn died on September 7, 2005, in Port Jervis at age 99.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Kathryn Elizabeth Altemeier Yohn. Memorabilia, #37-5-2100. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Box 1 of the collection was reprocessed at folder level in February and March 2026 by the Processing Archivist. Items from the original Box 1 were rehoused in a new Box 1, Box 5, Box 6, and Box 7.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Collection consists of scrapbook, correspondence, photographs, programs, publications, and ephemera documenting the campus life of Kathryn Elizabeth Yohn (née Altemeier), Cornell University Class of 1928. The materials reflect Yohn's academic career, participation in athletics, membership in the Alpha Omicron Pi sorority, and social life at Cornell.

controlaccess.subject: Athletics.
Women college students.
controlaccess.corpname: Alpha Omicron Pi. Epsilon Chapter (Cornell University)

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Materials in this series removed from original Box 1, reprocessed at folder level, and rehoused in new Box 1 and Box 5 in February-March 2026 by Processing Archivist.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series consists of scrapbook, correspondence, photographs, dance cards, cancelled checks and account book from the Tompkins County National Bank, course assignments, and ephemera related to Kathryn Yohn's personal life. Scrapbook contains correspondence, news clippings, event and performance programs, photographs, athletics tickets and programs, and other ephemera documenting Yohn's time at Cornell University. The correspondence includes letters to and from Yohn to friends, including a "round robin" letter exchange in which one person writes a letter, adds it to an envelope with previous letters in the exchange, and mails the envelope to the next person, who then adds their own letter. Also present are letters from Cornell offices regarding Yohn's academics. Dance cards are from on-campus formal social events and were used by Yohn to keep track of her dance partners at these functions. Series also includes an autograph book signed by Yohn's friends from Cornell; a progress report noting the number of academic credits Yohn had earned as of June 25, 1927; hand-colored maps of North America and the United States with handwritten legends, completed by Yohn as class assignments; and additional ephemera.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Scrapbook of Kathryn Elizabeth Yohn (née Altemeier), class of 1928, documenting her time at Cornell University. Materials date from 1924-1928. Includes correspondence, news clippings, event and performance programs, photographs, athletics tickets and programs, and other ephemera. Scrapbook begins with three pages titled "My Pals," where Yohn's Cornell friends wrote their names, hometown, the date, and a short note. The third page has additional names and notes written on the back. Correspondence documents Yohn's social life at Cornell and includes her letter of election to the Alpha Omicron Pi sorority; a letter from the Office of the Dean of Women confirming her appointment as president of the Alpha Omicron Pi house for the academic year 1927-1928; invitations to birthday parties, fraternity and sorority parties, performances, the Freshman Banquet (May 12, 1925), dances, tea, and other sorority functions; Valentine's Day cards; Christmas cards; and a letter from R. W. "Bob" Baker asking Yohn out on a date.

News clippings include coverage of sledding at Bebee Lake; the total solar eclipse of January 24, 1925, including images of the eclipse and an article from the New York Herald Tribune; Freshman gray cap burning; the contest for selecting the "Venetian Knight" during the Spring Day carnival (1925); Spring Day Gala Ball; a lecture by explorer Richard E. Byrd at Bailey Hall on March 28, 1927 where he discussed his expedition to the North Pole (includes ticket stub); and athletics including football, baseball, and the Women's Athletic Association.

Event programs are from concerts and performances on- and off-campus. On-campus events performances include the Cornell Dramatic Club's productions of "The Wash Tub" (1925, includes ticket stub) and a dance performance featuring Adolph Bolm, Ruth Page, and Maria Montero (1926); "Alice in Wonderland" presented by the women of the Class of 1928; the Department of Physical Education's Dance Festival (1925, 1926, 1927); the "Christening of Princess Cornellia," presumably a comedy skit (1925); Sophomore Cotillion (1925); Paul Whiteman and his Concert Orchestra at Bailey Hall (April 28, 1925); the Spring Day Hop (May 22, 1925 and May 21, 1926); Program of Kermis Plays for 1926: "Finding a Way Out" and "Amends" (February 12, 1926); a "Junior-Freshman Christening" in "the Baptistry of St. Willard" (Willard Straight Hall), likely a comedic performance; Cornell Glee, Banjo, and Mandolin Clubs Annual Senior Week Concert (1926); and the Athletic Conference of American College Women (ACACW) Official Program and Banquet at Cornell (1927). Programs for off-campus events include the Cornell Women's Dramatic Club performance of "The Dragon" at the Lyceum Theater in Ithaca (May 9, 1925); a charity performance of "Juanita" at the Lyceum Theater (1926); and the Savage Club of Ithaca performance of "Five Bewildered Freshmen" (May 19, 1927).

Photographs in the scrapbook depict Yohn; members of the Alpha Omicron Pi sorority; campus buildings; athletics events including the Cornell vs. Columbia football game (November 11, 1924) and rowing; and a party for women from the Class of 1928.

Athletics materials present include a Williams vs. Cornell football ticket stub (October 10, 1924); Rutgers vs. Cornell football ticket stub (October 18, 1925); Columbia vs. Cornell football program and ticket stub (November 1, 1924); a Winter Sports Season ticket valid for admission to all home basketball, wrestling, hockey, fencing, and lacrosse games and matches; Yale vs. Cornell Dual Track Meet program and ticket stubs (March 21, 1925); and Michigan vs. Cornell Track Meet (March 27, 1926).

Additional ephemera in the scrapbook include small dolls made from fabric, wire, and clothespins and associated with Yohn's sorority activities, including a "Doll Cabaret" performance given on October 7, 1925; Alpha Omicron Pi Initiation Banquet programs for 1925, 1926, and 1927; ribbons and pieces of fabric; tally cards from bridge games; a pennant reading "B-A-N-G"; St. Patrick's Day Formal dinner napkin or place mat; lace with pressed flower and ribbon; a permission slip allowing Yohn to be absent from her dorm overnight; Class of 1928 Memorial Fund donation slip; name tags from various events; and Women's Self Government Association (WSGA) ephemera.

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: Removed loose Venetian Knight handbill, small photograph, and memo book. Photograph and memo book placed in Series I (Box 5) and handbill in Series V (Box 7).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photograph of Cornell University Women's Athletic Association council, April 1927. Kathryn Altemeier is second from left in the bottom row.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Materials in this series removed from original Box 1, reprocessed at folder level, and rehoused in Box 6 in February-March 2026 by Processing Archivist.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series includes publications and ephemera related to Cornell academics, facilities, and official functions. These materials document Yohn's academic career at the university. Notable in this series are materials from Cornell's Sixtieth Annual Commencement on June 18, 1928, when Yohn graduated with her Bachelor of Arts in Education. Two Commencement programs are present, as are a program from the preceding Baccalaureate Service and a brochure of the Order of Events of the Week leading up to Commencement (June 12-18, 1928). Also included in this series are Yohn's copy of the Freshman Handbook, the Schedule of Examinations for academic year 1927-1928, a brochure for the newly-opened Willard Straight Hall, a Class of 1930 orientation schedule, and several buttons for the classes of 1925 and 1928.

did.unittitle: Competitive Examinations for State Scholarships in Cornell University
Cornell University Sixtieth Commencement Baccalaureate Service programs
Cornell University Sixtieth Annual Commencement programs
Cornell class button and Friday Luncheon card
Sixtieth Commencement of Cornell University Order of Events of the Week

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Materials in this series removed from original Box 1, reprocessed at folder level, and rehoused in Box 6 in February-March 2026 by Processing Archivist.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series includes programs for musical and theatrical performances by student groups at Cornell, as well as for additional performances on campus. Groups represented include the Cornell Glee, Banjo, and Mandolin Clubs; Cornell Dramatic Club; Kermis Club; Department of Physical Education; Cornell University Orchestra. Popular on-campus venues for these performances included Bailey Hall and Willard Straight Hall. Series also contains a program for the Cornell University Organ Recital at Sage Chapel. Performances frequently coincide with larger university events, including Junior Week, Senior Week, Spring Day, and Farmers' Week.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Materials in this series removed from original Box 1, reprocessed at folder level, and rehoused in Box 6 in February-March 2026 by Processing Archivist.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series includes programs, ticket stubs, clippings, and ephemera related to Cornell athletics. Yohn was active in several sports during her time on campus, and frequently attended home sporting events. This series documents her engagement with Cornell athletics. Bulk of series consists of programs and ticket stubs for Cornell sporting events, including football games, baseball games, and track meets. Visiting teams include St. Bonaventure, Niagara, Williams, Rutgers, Yale, Michigan, Colgate, Princeton, and Notre Dame. Athletics news clippings document rowing and the Women's Rifle Team, both of which Yohn participated in. Clippings include a photograph of Yohn with the Women's Rifle Team. Additional athletics materials include photographs of the rowing team [check this], Fall Sport Schedules for 1924, Cornell University Athletic Association Membership Coupon Book, and an invitation to a dinner hosted by the men's basketball team.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Materials in this series removed from original Box 1, reprocessed at folder level, and rehoused in Box 7 in February-March 2026 by Processing Archivist.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series contains clippings and ephemera related to student life at Cornell. Of note are materials from a contest to elect the "Venetian Knight," an honorary position during the Spring Day carnival in May 1925. That year's Spring Day festivities featured an evening carnival titled "So This is Venice!" themed after the Italian city. The individual elected as Venetian Knight would preside over this carnival. Unusually, the candidates for Venetian Knight were faculty members rather than students. The series includes a photograph of a rally likely held in support of one candidate, law professor Lyman P. Wilson. One of the photographs depicts rally participants wearing white Ku Klux Klan hoods and robes and is captioned "Wilson is a great favorite endorsed by the Klan." While the Klan had a presence in Ithaca around the time that the photograph was taken, the student dress and caption may not reflect formal ties to the organization. Also present is a postcard photograph of another rally in support of a candidate named Brown. Further Venetian Knight materials in this series include a handbill advertising the carnival; an admission ticket to the carnival, held on May 23, 1925; and a parody newspaper titled "the Venetian Knight Letter" published on the day of the carnival. Additional materials in this series include pamphlets and a clipping related to sorority life; a napkin from the Navy Day Ball on May 25, 1928; news clippings on student activities; and an itinerary of activities for an unspecified women's group.

did.unittitle: Senior Women's Ball banquet and tickets
"So This is Venice!" Spring Day carnival admission ticket

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Materials in this series removed from original Box 1, reprocessed at folder level, and rehoused in Box 7 in February-March 2026 by Processing Archivist.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series contains paper and fabric ephemera, including advertising and handmade items. Businesses represented by the advertising materials include the Barnes Hall Co-op; Rothschild Brothers department store; and Candy Land, a local ice cream parlor. Series also includes two items made of fabric, likely an armband and sash made by Yohn; additional small paper items, including two black squares of construction paper decorated with tissue paper tassels, resembling graduation caps; and a promotional score card for the 1925 Harold Lloyd film "The Freshman."

did.unittitle: Harold Lloyd in The Freshman score card
